title: quercus ocoteifolia f. podocarpa
intro: Quercus ocoteifolia f. podocarpa, also known as the Mexican evergreen oak, is a subspecies of evergreen tree native to the highlands of Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which can help to treat various health issues. The medicinal properties of this plant may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and sore throat, as well as support digestive health and boost the immune system. Quercus ocoteifolia f. podocarpa can be prepared as a tea or infused oil, and its bark, leaves, and roots can be used in herbal remedies to harness its health benefits.
